title: Welcome to Dart 🎯
description: Write your first Dart program and say hello to the world!

slides.title: What is Dart? 🤔
slides.content:
# The Language of Flutter

**Dart** is a modern, object-oriented programming language created by Google.

Think of Dart as the **engine** inside a car. If you want to build beautiful mobile apps using the Flutter framework (the car's body), you need Dart to make it run!

* It's easy to learn.
* It's incredibly fast.
* It's designed specifically for building user interfaces.

quizData.question: What is the relationship between Dart and Flutter?

options.text: Dart is the framework, Flutter is the language.
Dart is the language used to build Flutter apps.

quizData.explanation: Dart is the programming language that powers the Flutter framework.

slides.title: The Starting Line 🏁
slides.content:
## `main()`

Every Dart program must start somewhere. That "somewhere" is a special function called `main()`.

Think of `main()` as the **front door** to your app. When a user opens your app, Dart knocks on the front door and starts executing whatever is inside.

If you don't have a `main()` function, your program won't know where to start!

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Here is what an empty Dart program looks like. It doesn't do anything yet, but it's ready to go!

quizData.question: What is the entry point for every Dart application?
quizData.explanation: The main() function is the starting point for every Dart program.

slides.title: Hello, World! 🌍
slides.content:
## Making Dart Speak

To make Dart print text to the screen, we use the `print()` command.

Whatever text you put inside the parentheses `()` and wrap in quotes `''` will be displayed.

**Important:** Every instruction in Dart MUST end with a semicolon `;`. Think of the semicolon as the period at the end of a sentence.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Let's print a greeting to the world!

quizData.question:
Fill in the blank to print 'Progly is awesome!':
print('Progly is awesome!')_
quizData.explanation: Every statement in Dart must end with a semicolon.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Single vs Double Quotes

In Dart, you can use either single quotes `'Hello'` or double quotes `"Hello"` for text. Either one works perfectly fine!

However, the Dart community prefers **single quotes** for normal text, so it's a good habit to build early.
